seoTitle: How to Assign Tasks in a Microsoft Loop Task List
metaDescription: Assign owners and due dates to a Microsoft Loop task list, then sync assignments so they appear in each person's personal To Do or Planner view.
intro: Action items buried in meeting notes rarely get done. This guide shows how to build a task list in Microsoft Loop, assign clear ownership to each item, and connect the list to Microsoft To Do or Planner so assigned tasks actually show up where your teammates will see them.

steps.heading: Deploy a task list block
steps.body: Place your cursor on a free line in your Loop page and type the /task list slash command to drop a fresh action matrix onto the canvas.

steps.heading: Enter task names and due dates
steps.body: Type each task name and use the connected calendar picker to assign a specific due date.

steps.heading: Assign an owner to each task
steps.body: Click the empty field in the Assigned To column and select the right team member from the auto-populated list.

steps.heading: Sync to Planner or To Do
steps.body: Click the Task Apps indicator in the page header and choose to open the list in Planner to link it with the wider Microsoft 365 task ecosystem.

steps.heading: Publish and confirm
steps.body: Review the populated task matrix and publish the page so the assignments are live and visible to the whole team.

faq.question: Will assigned tasks show up in the person's own To Do app?
faq.answer: Assigning a task to someone in a Loop task list is designed to push that row into their personal 'Assigned to me' view once the sync is set up correctly.

faq.question: Can I switch a Loop task list to a board or calendar view?
faq.answer: Yes, you can change the layout of a task list between table, board, and calendar formats depending on how you want to review progress.

faq.question: What happens if a task doesn't sync to Planner or To Do?
faq.answer: This is usually because no user was assigned, since the assignment is what triggers the connection to a personal task tray.

faq.question: Do teammates need to open Loop to see their assigned tasks?
faq.answer: No, once synced, they can check tasks assigned to them directly from their personal Microsoft To Do or Planner app.
